rules:
You can play this card only if you discard another card from your hand.

Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Choose 1 or both:
• Shuffle up to 2 Pokémon from your discard pile into your deck.
• Shuffle up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Attach a Metal Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Metal Pokémon.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

The Retreat Cost of the Pokémon this card is attached to is ColorlessColorless less.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

attacks.name: Assault Tackle
attacks.effect: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Brave Blade
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Put an Energy attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon into their hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Each player shuffles their hand and puts it on the bottom of their deck. If either player put any cards on the bottom of their deck in this way, you draw 5 cards, and your opponent draws 4 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Attach a basic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

attacks.name: G-Max Fall
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Stone Gift
attacks.effect: Attach a Fighting Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon. If you do, heal 120 damage from that Pokémon.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Max Discharge
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max Pump
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Put an Energy attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon into their hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Each player shuffles their hand and puts it on the bottom of their deck. If either player put any cards on the bottom of their deck in this way, you draw 5 cards, and your opponent draws 4 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Attach a basic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

attacks.name: Beak Catch
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Spit Shot
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 160 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Swallow
attacks.effect: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Falling Down
attacks.effect: This Pokémon is now Asleep.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Assault Tackle
attacks.effect: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Brave Blade
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Lode Search
attacks.effect: Put a Trainer card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Crazy Claws
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Gritty Comeback
attacks.effect: Switch all damage counters on this Pokémon with those on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Shadow Bind
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Spark
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Electro Wheel
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, switch it with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Body Surf
attacks.effect: Attach a Water Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon. If you do, switch it with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Ocean Loop
attacks.effect: Put 2 Water Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Combustion Pillar
attacks.effect: Discard the top card of your deck. If that card is a Fire Energy card, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Steam Crush
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Anchor Anger
attacks.effect: If any of your Grass Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during their last turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Giga Hammer
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Giga Hammer.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

You can attach this card to 1 of your Pokémon only if you discard another card from your hand.

As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 10 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Put an Energy attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon into their hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

At the end of each turn, if the Pokémon this card is attached to has 3 or more damage counters on it, heal 30 damage from it and discard this card.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand. Your turn ends.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card onto the Basic Pokémon to evolve it, skipping the Stage 1. You can't use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

You can play this card only if you discard another card from your hand.

Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Pokémon, and it recovers from all Special Conditions.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Supporter card you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Shuffle up to 2 Supporter cards from your discard pile into your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Choose 1 or both:
• Shuffle up to 2 Pokémon from your discard pile into your deck.
• Shuffle up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Attach a Metal Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Metal Pokémon.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Each player shuffles their hand and puts it on the bottom of their deck. If either player put any cards on the bottom of their deck in this way, you draw 5 cards, and your opponent draws 4 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

At the end of each turn, if the Pokémon this card is attached to is affected by any Special Conditions, it recovers from all of them, and discard this card.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

If the Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, draw cards until you have 7 cards in your hand.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Heal 120 damage from 1 of your Pokémon that has at least 2 Energy attached. If you healed any damage in this way, discard 2 Energy from it.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Draw 3 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Pokémon you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Search your deck for an Evolution Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Move a basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Search your deck for a basic Energy card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Put up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

The Pokémon this card is attached to gets +30 HP.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Attach a basic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

The Retreat Cost of the Pokémon this card is attached to is ColorlessColorless less.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

attacks.name: Beak Catch
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Spit Shot
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 160 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Cotton Guard
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its majestic horns are meant only to impress the opposite gender. They never see use in battle.
Its curly fleece is such an effective cushion that this Pokémon could fall off a cliff and stand right back up at the bottom, unharmed.

attacks.name: Expand
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 10 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: If its fleece grows too long, Wooloo won't be able to move. Cloth made with the wool of this Pokémon is surprisingly strong.

attacks.name: Pluck
attacks.effect: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Smart enough to use tools in battle, these Pokémon have been seen picking up rocks and flinging them or using ropes to wrap up enemies.
It will bravely challenge any opponent, no matter how powerful. This Pokémon benefits from every battle—even a defeat increases its strength a bit.

attacks.name: Dragon Pulse
attacks.effect: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.

flavorText: The mountains it calls home are nearly two miles in height. On rare occasions, it descends to play with the children living in the towns below.
It knows the forest inside and out. If it comes across a wounded Pokémon, Oranguru will gather medicinal herbs to treat it.

attacks.name: Energy Assist
attacks.effect: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body secretes oil that this Pokémon spreads over its nest as a coating to protect it from dust. Cinccino won't tolerate even a speck of the stuff.
The way it brushes away grime with its tail can be helpful when cleaning. But its focus on spotlessness can make cleaning more of a hassle.

attacks.name: Glance
attacks.effect: Look at the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Tail Slap
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: The way it brushes away grime with its tail can be helpful when cleaning. But its focus on spotlessness can make cleaning more of a hassle.

attacks.name: Carry Off
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. They shuffle that Pokémon and all attached cards into their deck. Then, shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.

flavorText: When it needs to think, it rotates its head 180 degrees to sharpen its intellectual power.

attacks.name: Send Back
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It begins to hoot at the same time every day. Some Trainers use them in place of clocks.

attacks.name: G-Max Fall
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Swallow
attacks.effect: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Falling Down
attacks.effect: This Pokémon is now Asleep.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: It is not satisfied unless it eats over 880 pounds of food every day. When it is done eating, it goes promptly to sleep.

attacks.name: Assault Tackle
attacks.effect: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Brave Blade
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dig Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Muscular Nose
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has 8 or more damage counters on it, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: These Pokémon live in herds. Their trunks have incredible grip strength, strong enough to crush giant rocks into powder.

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: If a job requires serious strength, this Pokémon will excel at it. Its copper body tarnishes in the rain, turning a vibrant green color.

attacks.name: Iron Wings
attacks.effect: You may discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, during your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 100 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: This Pokémon reigns supreme in the skies of the Galar region. The black luster of its steel body could drive terror into the heart of any foe.

attacks.name: Charge Order
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your Benched Pawniard.

flavorText: Violent conflicts erupt between Bisharp and Fraxure over places where sharpening stones can be found.
A pack of these Pokémon forms to serve a Bisharp boss. Each Pawniard trains diligently, dreaming of one day taking the lead.

attacks.name: Damage Rush
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Living in mud with a high iron content has given it a strong steel body.

attacks.name: Triple Smash
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Energy Slap
attacks.effect: You may move all Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon scrapes its spikes across rocks, and then uses the tips of its feelers to absorb the nutrients it finds within the stone.
It defends itself by launching spikes, but its aim isn't very good at first. Only after a lot of practice will it improve.

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It chomps with its gaping mouth. Its huge jaws are actually steel horns that have been transformed.
What appears to be an iron helmet is actually hardened hair. This Pokémon lives for the thrill of battle.

attacks.name: Hone Claws
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Slash attack does 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Living with a savage, seafaring people has toughened this Pokémon's body so much that parts of it have turned to iron.

attacks.name: Thief
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand. Choose a card you find there and put it on the bottom of their deck.

flavorText: It secretly marks potential targets with a scent. By following the scent, it stalks its targets and steals from them when they least expect it.

attacks.name: Instigate
attacks.effect: Your opponent shuffles their hand and puts it on the bottom of their deck. If they put any cards on the bottom of their deck in this way, they draw 3 cards.

flavorText: Aided by the soft pads on its feet, it silently raids the food stores of other Pokémon. It survives off its ill-gotten gains.

attacks.name: Poison Claws
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It bounces toward opponents and gouges them with poisonous claws. No more than a scratch is needed to knock out its adversaries.
It makes frightening noises with its poison-filled cheek sacs. When opponents flinch, Croagunk hits them with a poison jab.

attacks.name: Hard Press
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Toxic Strike
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It's so vicious that it's called the Sand Demon. Yet when confronted by Hippowdon, Drapion keeps a low profile and will never pick a fight.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It attacks using the claws on its tail. Once locked in its grip, its prey is unable to move as this Pokémon's poison seeps in.

attacks.name: Lode Search
attacks.effect: Put a Trainer card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Crazy Claws
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Obstruct
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.

flavorText: It evolved after experiencing numerous fights. While crossing its arms, it lets out a shout that would make any opponent flinch.

attacks.name: Night Slash
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This very aggressive Pokémon will recklessly challenge opponents stronger than itself.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Thought to be the oldest form of Zigzagoon, it moves in zigzags and wreaks havoc upon its surroundings.

attacks.name: Stone Gift
attacks.effect: Attach a Fighting Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon. If you do, heal 120 damage from that Pokémon.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Wild Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It stands in grasslands, watching the sun's descent from zenith to horizon. This Pokémon has a talent for delivering dynamic kicks.

attacks.name: Octolock
attacks.effect: Until this Grapploct leaves the Active Spot, the Defending Pokémon's attacks cost ColorlessColorless more, and the Defending Pokémon can't retreat. This effect can't be applied more than once.

attacks.name: Tough Swing
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: A body made up of nothing but muscle makes the grappling moves this Pokémon performs with its tentacles tremendously powerful.
It's very curious, but its means of investigating things is to try to punch them with its tentacles. The search for food is what brings it onto land.

attacks.name: Bind
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It's very curious, but its means of investigating things is to try to punch them with its tentacles. The search for food is what brings it onto land.

attacks.name: Power Press
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra Fighting Energy attached (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: When it contracts its body, over 220 pounds of sand sprays from its nose. If it ever runs out of sand, it becomes disheartened.

attacks.name: Coil
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's attacks do 120 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its unique style of coiling allows it to blast sand out of its sand sac more efficiently.
As it digs, it swallows sand and stores it in its neck pouch. The pouch can hold more than 17 pounds of sand.

attacks.name: Sand Attack
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

flavorText: It spews sand from its nostrils. While the enemy is blinded, it burrows into the ground to hide.

attacks.name: Double Impact
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 120 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Mud that hardens around a Mudsdale's legs sets harder than stone. It's so hard that it allows this Pokémon to scrap a truck with a single kick.
Loads weighing up to 50 times as much as its own body weight pose no issue for this Pokémon. It's skilled at making use of mud.

attacks.name: Raging Hammer
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rocky Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Explosion
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 120 damage to itself.

flavorText: This mysterious Pokémon started life as an ancient clay figurine made over 20,000 years ago.
It moves while spinning around on its single foot. Some Baltoy have been seen spinning on their heads.
It moves while spinning around on its single foot. Some Baltoy have been seen spinning on their heads.

attacks.name: Double Draw
attacks.effect: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: If a tree branch shakes when there is no wind, it's a Sudowoodo, not a tree. It hides from the rain.

attacks.name: Rock Tumble
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Bedrock Shake
attacks.effect: This attack also does 60 damage to each Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It relies on its carapace to deflect incoming attacks and throw its enemy off balance. As soon as that happens, it drives its drill into the foe.

attacks.name: Rock Slide
attacks.effect: This attack also does 10 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Protected by an armor-like hide, it is capable of living in molten lava of 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit.

attacks.name: Stomp Off
attacks.effect: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: Strong, but not too bright, this Pokémon can shatter even a skyscraper with its charging tackles.
It can remember only one thing at a time. Once it starts rushing, it forgets why it started.

attacks.name: Coordinated Beatdown
attacks.effect: If Hitmonlee is on your Bench, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Its punches slice the air. However, it seems to need a short break after fighting for three minutes.

attacks.name: Coordinated Strike
attacks.effect: If Hitmonchan is on your Bench, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: The legs freely contract and stretch. The stretchy legs allow it to hit a distant foe with a rising kick.
A team of Diglett triplets. It triggers huge earthquakes by burrowing 60 miles underground.
If a Diglett digs through a field, it leaves the soil perfectly tilled and ideal for planting crops.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Teatime
attacks.effect: Each player draws 2 cards.

attacks.name: Poltergeist
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 50 damage for each Trainer card you find there.

flavorText: This species lives in antique teapots. Most pots are forgeries, but on rare occasions, an authentic work is found.

attacks.name: Teatime
attacks.effect: Each player draws 2 cards.

flavorText: This Pokémon is said to have been born when a lonely spirit possessed a cold, leftover cup of tea.

attacks.name: Sleepy Pulse
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep. During Pokémon Checkup, your opponent flips 2 coins instead of 1. If either of them is tails, that Pokémon is still Asleep.

attacks.name: Super Hypnoblast
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Asleep, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: It drowses and dreams all the time. It's best to leave it be if it's just woken up, as it's a terrible grump when freshly roused from sleep.

attacks.name: Moonlight
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It eats dreams and releases mist. The mist is pink when it's eating a good dream, and black when it's eating a nightmare.

attacks.name: Gritty Comeback
attacks.effect: Switch all damage counters on this Pokémon with those on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Shadow Bind
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: On the night of a full moon, if shadows move on their own and laugh, it must be Gengar's doing.

attacks.name: Nightmare
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Its tongue is made of gas. If licked, its victim starts shaking constantly until death eventually comes.

attacks.name: Fade Out
attacks.effect: Put this Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.

flavorText: Born from gases, anyone would faint if engulfed by its gaseous body, which contains poison.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Brave and prideful, this Pokémon dashes airily through the forest, its steps aided by the psychic power stored in the fur on its fetlocks.

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: This Pokémon will look into your eyes and read the contents of your heart. If it finds evil there, it promptly hides away.

attacks.name: Max Discharge
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Spark
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Electro Wheel
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, switch it with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Attack the Wound
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: As it eats the seeds stored up in its pocket-like pouches, this Pokémon is not just satisfying its constant hunger. It's also generating electricity.

attacks.name: Shocking Needles
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads. If at least 2 of them are heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It feeds on seaweed, using its teeth to scrape it off rocks. Electric current flows from the tips of its spines.

attacks.name: Electrodash
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: This Pokémon generates electricity and channels it into its legs to keep them going strong. Boltund can run nonstop for three full days.

attacks.name: Big Bite
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Fighting Fangs
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V or Pokémon-GX, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon generates electricity and channels it into its legs to keep them going strong. Boltund can run nonstop for three full days.
This Pokémon is very popular as a herding dog in the Galar region. As it runs, it generates electricity from the base of its tail.

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon is very popular as a herding dog in the Galar region. As it runs, it generates electricity from the base of its tail.

attacks.name: Spike Draw
attacks.effect: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Thunderous Bolt
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Volt Wave
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It launches electrified fur from its abdomen as its means of attack. Opponents hit by the fur could be in for three full days and nights of paralysis.
Joltik can be found clinging to other Pokémon. It's soaking up static electricity because it can't produce a charge on its own.

attacks.name: Strobe Shock
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand. If you find any Energy cards there, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: This Pokémon flashes a bright light that blinds its prey. This creates an opening for it to deliver an electrical attack.

attacks.name: Luring Glow
attacks.effect: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its antennae, which evolved from a fin, have both positive and negative charges flowing through them.
On the dark ocean floor, its only means of communication is its constantly flashing lights.
Its long tail serves as a ground to protect itself from its own high-voltage power.

attacks.name: Charge
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Pikachu that can generate powerful electricity have cheek sacs that are extra soft and super stretchy.
It shows no mercy to any who desecrate fields and mountains. It will fly around on its icy wings, causing a blizzard to chase offenders away.
It eats snow that piles up on the ground. The more snow it eats, the bigger and more impressive the spikes on its back grow.

attacks.name: Water Arrow
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It's so strong that it can knock out some opponents in a single hit, but it also may forget what it's battling midfight.

attacks.name: Vise Bite
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Jaw Lock
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: This Pokémon rapidly extends its retractable neck to sink its sharp fangs into distant enemies and take them down.
It starts off battles by attacking with its rock-hard horn, but as soon as the opponent flinches, this Pokémon bites down and never lets go.

attacks.name: Silent Shot
attacks.effect: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Hydro Snipe
attacks.effect: You may put an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon into their hand.

flavorText: It has many hidden capabilities, such as fingertips that can shoot water and a membrane on its back that it can use to glide through the air.

attacks.name: Aqua Bullet
attacks.effect: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It has many hidden capabilities, such as fingertips that can shoot water and a membrane on its back that it can use to glide through the air.
A clever combatant, this Pokémon battles using water balloons created with moisture secreted from its palms.
A clever combatant, this Pokémon battles using water balloons created with moisture secreted from its palms.
When scared, this Pokémon cries. Its tears pack the chemical punch of 100 onions, and attackers won't be able to resist weeping.
When scared, this Pokémon cries. Its tears pack the chemical punch of 100 onions, and attackers won't be able to resist weeping.

attacks.name: Secret Sword
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Water Reserve
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Water Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: If it builds up enough speed swimming, it can jump out above the waves and glide for over 300 feet.

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: The small spikes covering its body developed from scales. They inject a toxin that causes fainting.

attacks.name: G-Max Pump
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Body Surf
attacks.effect: Attach a Water Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon. If you do, switch it with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Ocean Loop
attacks.effect: Put 2 Water Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Aqua Wash
attacks.effect: You may put 2 Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon into their hand.

flavorText: A smart and kindhearted Pokémon, it glides across the surface of the sea while its beautiful song echoes around it.

attacks.name: Ripping Horn
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Using its horn, it bores holes in riverbed boulders, making nests to prevent its eggs from washing away.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

flavorText: Its dorsal, pectoral, and tail fins wave elegantly in water. That is why it is known as the Water Dancer.
Its dorsal and pectoral fins are strongly developed like muscles. It can swim at a speed of five knots.

attacks.name: Heavy Pincers
attacks.effect: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Claw Rend
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: The large and hard pincer has 10,000-horsepower strength. However, being so big, it is unwieldy to move.

attacks.name: Super Slice
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It can be found near the sea. The large pincers grow back if they are torn out of their sockets.

attacks.name: Aqua Shower
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It can be found near the sea. The large pincers grow back if they are torn out of their sockets.

attacks.name: Shell Grab
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its shell is extremely hard. It cannot be shattered, even with a bomb. The shell opens only when it is attacking.

attacks.name: Water Splash
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It swims facing backward by opening and closing its two-piece shell. It is surprisingly fast.

attacks.name: Hundred Foot Flames
attacks.effect: For each Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon, discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: When it heats up, its body temperature reaches about 1,500 degrees Fahrenheit. It lashes its body like a whip and launches itself at enemies.

attacks.name: Relentless Flames
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It stores flammable gas in its body and uses it to generate heat. The yellow sections on its belly get particularly hot.
It stores flammable gas in its body and uses it to generate heat. The yellow sections on its belly get particularly hot.

attacks.name: Flame Cloak
attacks.effect: Attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Bright Flame
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It juggles a pebble with its feet, turning it into a burning soccer ball. Its shots strike opponents hard and leave them scorched.

attacks.name: Pyro Ball
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Burning Kick
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It juggles a pebble with its feet, turning it into a burning soccer ball. Its shots strike opponents hard and leave them scorched.

attacks.name: Flare Striker
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It's skilled at both offense and defense, and it gets pumped up when cheered on. But if it starts showboating, it could put itself in a tough spot.
Its thick and fluffy fur protects it from the cold and enables it to use hotter fire moves.

attacks.name: Flame Charge
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Fire Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It kicks berries right off the branches of trees and then juggles them with its feet, practicing its footwork.
A warm-up of running around gets fire energy coursing through this Pokémon's body. Once that happens, it's ready to fight at full power.

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It has special pads on the backs of its feet, and one on its nose. Once it's raring to fight, these pads radiate tremendous heat.

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Explosive substances coat the shell on its back. Enemies that dare attack it will be blown away by an immense detonation.

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Bright Flame
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Only female Salazzle exist. They emit a gas laden with pheromones to captivate male Salandit.

attacks.name: Smog
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Its venom sacs produce a fluid that this Pokémon then heats up with the flame in its tail. This process creates Salandit's poisonous gas.

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: There's a hole in its tail that allows it to draw in the air it needs to keep its fire burning. If the hole gets blocked, this Pokémon will fall ill.

attacks.name: Spreading Flames
attacks.effect: Attach up to 3 Fire Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Energy Burst
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Combustion Pillar
attacks.effect: Discard the top card of your deck. If that card is a Fire Energy card, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Steam Crush
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Flame Cloak
attacks.effect: Attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is said to live 1,000 years, and each of its tails is loaded with supernatural powers.
While young, it has six gorgeous tails. When it grows, several new tails are sprouted.

attacks.name: Blessing of Fluff
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Grass Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: The cotton on the head of this Pokémon can be spun into a glossy, gorgeous yarn—a Galar regional specialty.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It whirls around in the wind while singing a joyous song. This delightful display has charmed many into raising this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Brainwave
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Psychic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It emits psychic energy to observe and study what's around it—and what's around it can include things over six miles away.

attacks.name: Reflect
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 40 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: As it grows inside its shell, it uses its psychic abilities to monitor the outside world and prepare for evolution.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: A constant collector of information, this Pokémon is very smart. Very strong is what it isn't.
Often found in gardens, this Pokémon has hairs on its body that it uses to assess its surroundings.

attacks.name: Drum Roll
attacks.effect: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Drum Beating
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Drum Beating.

flavorText: By drumming, it taps into the power of its special tree stump. The roots of the stump follow its direction in battle.
By drumming, it taps into the power of its special tree stump. The roots of the stump follow its direction in battle.

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: The faster a Thwackey can beat out a rhythm with its two sticks, the more respect it wins from its peers.

attacks.name: Taunt
attacks.effect: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Double Hit
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.

flavorText: The faster a Thwackey can beat out a rhythm with its two sticks, the more respect it wins from its peers.
When it uses its special stick to strike up a beat, the sound waves produced carry revitalizing energy to the plants and flowers in the area.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: When it uses its special stick to strike up a beat, the sound waves produced carry revitalizing energy to the plants and flowers in the area.

attacks.name: Anchor Anger
attacks.effect: If any of your Grass Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during their last turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Giga Hammer
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Giga Hammer.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Bite Together
attacks.effect: If Durant is on your Bench, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: They lay their eggs deep inside their nests. When attacked by Heatmor, they retaliate using their massive mandibles.

attacks.name: Powerful Needles
attacks.effect: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.

flavorText: With noises that could be mistaken for the rattles of maracas, it creates an upbeat rhythm, startling bird Pokémon and making them fly off in a hurry.

attacks.name: Cotton Ride
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent shuffles their Active Pokémon and all attached cards into their deck.

flavorText: It scatters cotton all over the place as a prank. If it gets wet, it'll become too heavy to move and have no choice but to answer for its mischief.
It shoots cotton from its body to protect itself. If it gets caught up in hurricane-strength winds, it can get sent to the other side of the Earth.

attacks.name: Paralyze Poison
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is also Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: After captivating opponents with its sweet scent, it lashes them with its thorny whips.

attacks.name: Sweet Scent
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Its flowers give off a relaxing fragrance. The stronger its aroma, the healthier the Roselia is.

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its flowers give off a relaxing fragrance. The stronger its aroma, the healthier the Roselia is.

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Line Force
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
